brief_document.title: Non-resident signal delivery and terminal/unknown handling
brief_document.purpose: Make signals durable regardless of whether the target process is currently resident. A signal sent while a workflow is momentarily suspended or being restored must still be recorded and delivered once the workflow is resident again — no external event is silently dropped. A signal to a terminal or unknown workflow must fail cleanly with a typed error.
brief_document.task: Extend the SignalRouter so that when the target process is NonResident, the signal is still recorded as SignalReceived and queued for delivery on resume via the engine seam; and when the target is Terminal or Unknown, the router records nothing and returns a typed error. Implement the resume handoff path so a recorded-but-undelivered signal reaches the mailbox once AE makes the process resident.

requirements.title: Record-and-defer for non-resident targets
requirements.spec: WHEN a signal targets a workflow whose process is NonResident, THE SYSTEM SHALL still append SignalReceived AND register the signal for delivery to the mailbox once AE makes the process resident.
acceptance: resume.rs handles the NonResident branch: the SignalReceived event is recorded via the workflow's single Recorder (the engine-seam record method, AT-001 — never direct EventStore::append) so it is durable / replayable, and the signal is registered with the seam for delivery-on-resume.
The router does not fail or drop the signal when the target is NonResident (CO7 — no silent drop).
A test marks a workflow NonResident, routes a signal, asserts SignalReceived is appended and the signal is registered for resume delivery (observable via the fake seam).
A follow-up in the same test transitions the workflow to Resident, drives the resume handoff, and asserts the mailbox receives the deferred signal.

requirements.title: Resume handoff delivers deferred signals in order
requirements.spec: WHEN AE makes a previously non-resident workflow resident, THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver any recorded-but-undelivered signals to its mailbox, in the order they were recorded.
acceptance: The resume handoff delivers deferred signals to the mailbox in recorded order (the SignalReceived sequence order).

The deferred-delivery tracking (which recorded-but-undelivered signals remain) is IN-MEMORY engine-runtime state, not a durable structure: durability is already provided by the recorded SignalReceived event in the store. Within one engine lifetime the in-memory set prevents a second live delivery; ACROSS a restart the in-memory set is discarded and is not relied upon — a restored workflow re-reaches its receive through AD replay, which returns the recorded SignalReceived from history. A doc comment states this so no one mistakes the in-memory set for the durability mechanism.

Once delivered live, a deferred signal is not delivered a second time on a subsequent resume within the same engine lifetime; on a full replay after restart, AD — not this path — returns the recorded signal.
A test defers two signals for a NonResident workflow, marks it Resident, runs the handoff, and asserts both arrive in recorded order exactly once.

requirements.title: Terminal/unknown target returns a typed error, records nothing
requirements.spec: IF a signal targets a workflow that is Terminal or Unknown, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return a typed error AND SHALL NOT append any SignalReceived event.
acceptance: Routing to a Terminal workflow returns a typed SignalError (e.g. WorkflowTerminal) and appends nothing.
Routing to an Unknown workflow returns a typed SignalError (e.g. WorkflowNotFound) and appends nothing.
The error type is a thiserror enum in this module's error surface (no anyhow, CO5).
Tests assert: terminal target -> typed error + zero events appended; unknown target -> typed error + zero events appended.

boundaries: SHALL NOT manage workflow residency/lifecycle itself — it reacts to seam-reported residency and registers deferred delivery; AE owns the transition to Resident.
SHALL NOT implement replay of deferred signals — AD returns recorded signals on a full restore; this brief covers live resume handoff only.
SHALL NOT change the resident happy-path delivery from AT-005 beyond adding the non-resident/terminal/unknown branches.
verification: cargo test -p aion passes, including non-resident and terminal/unknown tests.
No SignalReceived is appended for terminal/unknown targets (verified by test).
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: Signalling a workflow whose process is not currently resident still records SignalReceived; the signal is delivered to the mailbox once AE makes the process resident.
Signalling a terminal or unknown workflow returns a typed error and records nothing.

stories.text: As a workflow author, I want a signal sent while my workflow was momentarily not resident to still reach me so that no external event is silently dropped.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny across the cluster's modules.
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
Library errors use thiserror domain types; no anyhow in library code; no unwrap/expect in library code; lock poisoning handled explicitly.

These modules live in the aion crate. The seam with AE (workflow process lifecycle, supervision, module loading, residency resolution) is via an explicit engine-facing handle/trait this cluster consumes; this cluster does not manage process lifecycle.

Every interaction that changes what a workflow observed is recorded as an aion-core Event before/at the point of delivery; queries record nothing. No code path delivers a recordable observation without recording it (no silent observation).

Durable timers go through EventStore::schedule_timer / expired_timers; all AT event appends (TimerStarted/Fired/Cancelled, SignalReceived, ChildWorkflow*) go through the target workflow's single Recorder via the engine seam (AD CO6) — never a direct EventStore::append and no bespoke side-channel persistence outside the aion-store contract.

Tests run against aion-store's InMemoryStore; timer expiry, signal delivery + durability, query reply, child spawn, and all/race/map each have explicit test coverage. Time-dependent tests inject the clock — no wall-clock sleeps in tests.
Timer poll intervals, query timeouts, and recovery-tick cadence are engine-configured, never hardcoded defaults (per the no-assumed-defaults rule).

resolved_context.intention: Define the engine-side mechanisms that let a running workflow process interact with the outside world and coordinate concurrent work: durable timers, signal delivery, read-only queries, child-workflow spawning, and the all/race/map concurrency primitives. A workflow process sleeps for a month and wakes; an external system signals an awaiting workflow and the workflow continues in microseconds; an operator queries a live workflow without perturbing it; a parent spawns children and fans work out across the BEAM. These are the interaction surfaces, and they must be both microsecond-fast on the live path and exactly recoverable after a crash. Every interaction that changes what a workflow observed (a timer firing, a signal arriving, a child completing) is recorded as an aion-core Event so that AD's replay returns the same observation without re-waiting; every interaction that observes without changing (a query) is never recorded. The work here is the live half of durable execution — recording the observable, delivering it over beamr's native mailbox and timer wheel, and recovering it on restart — paired with AD's replay half that reads those same events back.

provenance.quote: Define the engine-side mechanisms that let a running workflow process interact with the outside world and coordinate concurrent work: durable timers, signal delivery, read-only queries, child-workflow
